frontmatter.description: Pairing sessions over video should be capped at 90 minutes; longer sessions degrade decision quality and the recorded artifacts tend to be unusable.

asset.body:
Pairing sessions over video should be capped at 90 minutes.

Observations from the last quarter:

- Sessions over 90 minutes produce decisions that are revisited at the next standup in 70% of cases.
- Recordings over 90 minutes are rarely watched back; the index file is too dense to be useful.
- Splitting a planned 3-hour session into two 90-minute halves with a 30-minute solo gap produces noticeably better followup notes.

This is partly subjective and partly observed from our retro data. The 90-minute number is rough — anywhere between 75 and 100 minutes shows the same pattern.

feedback.reason: Used to justify splitting the April planning session in half
The 90-minute cap was too tight for a deep-dive design session
feedback.note: Probably want a separate carve-out for design vs implementation pairing.

humanGrade.rationale: Useful but soft — partly subjective, with one positive and one substantive negative pointing at a missing nuance. A calibrated judge should request human review rather than commit to a binary accept/reject.
